findings.evidence: `rg` hits: `150:def OSet`, `155:def XSet`, and lines 189-206 prove the four wrapper theorems as `G.O.existsUnique_row_of_column c`, `G.X.existsUnique_row_of_column c`, `G.O.existsUnique_column_of_row r`, `G.X.existsUnique_column_of_row r` with no explicit `[OSet]`/`[XSet]` rewrite.
findings.fix: Use explicit wrapper proofs such as `by simpa [OSet] using G.O.existsUnique_row_of_column c` and `by simpa [XSet] using G.X.existsUnique_column_of_row r` for all four lemmas.
findings.issue: `existsUnique_ORow_of_column`, `existsUnique_XRow_of_column`, `existsUnique_OColumn_of_row`, and `existsUnique_XColumn_of_row` close by assigning the underlying `pointSet` lemmas directly, relying on `OSet`/`XSet` being definitionally equal to `G.O.pointSet`/`G.X.pointSet`.

summary: The proofs are mostly direct, but the wrapper uniqueness lemmas rely on undocumented definitional unfolding of `OSet`/`XSet`. Make those wrapper reductions explicit so later changes to the set definitions do not silently break the API proofs.
